Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Or Tax Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.S Costs
Off Calendar May-20-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Benjamin D. Braly of BARON & BUDD, P.C. (214-521-3605), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Beverly Del Pino, individually and as successor-in-interst to James Del Pino, deceased, Nicole Del Pino, Michelle Voves
Sara Kristin Egide of TUCKER ELLIS LLP (415-617-2400), appearing for defendant / opposing party Rockwell Automation, Inc.

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS BEVERLY I DEL PINO, ET AL.'S MOTION TO STRIKE OR TAX DEFENDANT ROCKWELL AUTOMATION, INC.'S COSTS

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to tax defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.'s memorandum of costs is granted in part and denied in part. Of the $57,240.91 defendant claimed, $5,655 is taxed and defendant is awarded $51,585.91 as follows: Plaintiffs do not challenge the $2,230 claimed for filing and motion fees. Of the $10,879.68 claimed for deposition costs, plaintiffs only challenge the costs related to the depositions of Cutler-Hammer's PMK and Wolfgang Knauss. Plaintiffs' motion is denied as to these challenges as the costs are explicitly allowed and were incurred, reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation rather than merely convenient or beneficial to its preparation, and reasonable in amount. Thus the motion is denied as to the full claim for $10,879.68. As to the $44,131.23 claimed for expert witness costs pursuant to C.C.P. 998, Rockwell's offer of a dismissal without prejudice in exchange for a mutual waiver of costs was reasonable and made in good faith and the court uses is discretion to award some of these costs. Of these costs, plaintiffs only challenge hours related to Bifareti Construction, GradCo and Wolfgang Knauss. As to Bifareti Construction, the recoverable hours are reduced to 30 at an hourly rate of $195 and thus $5,655 is taxed and $5,850 is allowed. As to GradCo and Mr. Knauss, plaintiffs' motion is denied and the claimed costs are awarded. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

Off Calendar Jan-16-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Benjamin D. Braly of BARON & BUDD, P.C. (800-222-2766), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties James Del Pino / Beverly Del Pino, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT EATON CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Eaton Corporation, individually and as successor-in-interest to Cutler-Hammer, Inc., and Eaton Electrical, Inc. Erroneously sued herein as SII to Westinghouse Electric, LLC's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 through 5 and granted as to issue 6 (punitive damages). As to summary judgment and adjudication issues 1 through 5, defendant sustained its initial burden. The deposition testimony of Mr. Del Pino and Mr. Boness create a triable issue whether Mr. Del Pino was exposed to Cutler-Hammer asbestos-containing contactors and arc shields. As to summary adjudication of punitive damages, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to submit admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Defendant's objection to plaintiffs' Exhibit K is sustained. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

Darin A. Leviloff of HOWARD ROME MARTIN & RIDLEY LLP (650-365-7715, X211), appearing for defendant / joinder Eaton Corporation, individually and as SII to Cutler-Hammer, Inc., and Eaton Electrical, Inc. erroneously sued herein as SII to Westinghouse Electric, LLC
Christine M. Tamer of BARON & BUDD, P.C. (214-521-3605), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties James Del Pino, et al.
(Refer to sign in sheet for all other appearances.)

RULING:
DEFENDANT RSCC WIRE & CABLE LLC FKA ROCKBESTOS-SUPRENANT CABLE CORPORATION'S MOTION TO STAY OR DISMISS PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT BASED UPON FORUM NON CONVENIENS, AND MOTIONS BY JOINDERS

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant RSCC Wire & Cable LLC's motion to dismiss the action on the grounds of forum non conveniens is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that Texas is a suitable alternative forum because it did not show that all remaining defendants are subject to jurisdiction there. (See Stangvik v. Shiley, Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744; American Cemwood Corp. v. American Home Assurance Co. (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 431.) Defendant's alternate motion to stay the action is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that California is a seriously inconvenient forum as the balance of private and public interest factors makes it just that the litigation remain in California. All motions by joinders are also denied. Defendant's motion is denied without prejudice. An Order re: RSCC's and all Joinders' motions was signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Erin F. Robinson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 12199 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preferential Trial Setting
Off Calendar Jun-19-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted per CCP 36(d). Trial is October 1, 2012 at 11:15am in Department 503. Plaintiff stipulates that summary motions may be heard on 15 days notice without an additional two days for e-service, with oppositions due 5 court days before the hearing and replies due 2 court days before the hearing. Last day for hearing summary motions depends on the first letter of the first name of the party. For defendants with letters A-G last day is Sept. 13, H-O is Sept. 18, P-Z is Sept. 20. The time to respond to written contention discovery is shortened to 20 days. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on all defendants no later than Aug 31, 2012. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm Sept. 20, 2012. Fact discovery cut-off date is Sept. 14, 2012. Expert discovery cut off date is Sept. 28, 2012. No later than July 2, 2012, plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than July 10, 2012, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Plaintiff should provide information of any bankruptcy claims to all defendants within 3 Court days of filing. Expert pared down list should be completed 2 weeks before the trial. The parties to meet and confer regarding any other scheduling or other issue which any of them want to have included in the preference order. Further case management conference on July 24, 2012 at 1:30pm. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to S. F. Local Rules of Court 5. Parties must submit statements before the conference and appear by persons with full authorities to settle. Client must be present at the conference or seek approval by the Court to be on telephone standby. Prevailing party to prepare an order. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Not Reported (503/TLJ)
